behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is explicitly about translation quality (grammar + meaning preservation), which depends on real model output. A stubbed translator would hide failures in model judgments, so Unit/Integration cannot catch the real failure boundary. It is not Workflow because only one model decision is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation model on a fixed multilingual evaluation set and score outputs for adequacy/fluency (or human-labeled pass/fail). Fail if quality drops below threshold or semantic preservation checks fail.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration: API client error handling -> fallback translator -> caller contract. A deterministic in-process fake API returning 503 is sufficient to reproduce this specific failure mode. System was considered but rejected because behavior text only requires handling a 503 response, not real network/timeout/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client abstraction + dictionary translator with a fake API adapter that returns 503 and a real fallback component; assert HTTP/service response is success with fallback translation and no propagated upstream error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level (System) rejected by behavior text: only 'returns a 503 error' is required; no real network condition is specified.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on real interaction between fallback logic and SQLite persistence (query/schema/data mapping). Mocking DB access can hide SQL/query wiring failures. System is unnecessary because local SQLite usage is exactly an Integration-level local DB boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Use a test SQLite database seeded with known phrase pairs, invoke fallback translator for seeded phrases, and assert exact expected translations are returned from DB-backed lookups.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level (System) rejected by behavior text: boundary is local SQLite lookup, not subprocess/real network/OS event behavior.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Although edit distance computation alone is Unit-testable, this behavior explicitly includes persistence ('stores the metric'), so the real failure boundary is compute + repository handoff. Agentic/System are not required because correctness here is deterministic string processing plus local storage.
behaviors.test_description: Execute translation flow with deterministic primary/fallback strings, verify computed character-level edit distance matches expected value, and assert the metric row is persisted with correct fields in test storage.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level (Agentic) rejected by behavior text: requirement is deterministic edit-distance computation/storage, not model-output quality.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state behavior: prefer primary output while asynchronously recording comparison. It can be caught by wiring modules with deterministic translator stubs and verifying response ordering plus background side effect. Agentic was considered but rejected because the behavior text tests selection/logging flow, not quality of model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translator components enabled (stubbed deterministic outputs), assert caller receives primary translation immediately, then assert background job/log/metric write for primary-vs-fallback comparison completes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level (Agentic) rejected by behavior text: only 'returns the primary translation and logs comparison' is required; no assertion on model semantic correctness.
